record_identity.title: Deception-adjacent classification and action-pathway attribution discipline

summary: This patch note records the Caelestis Architecture repair response to VIGIL-2026-FM-0025, VIGIL-2026-FM-0026, and VIGIL-2026-FM-0027. The repair adds Deception-Adjacent Classification Collapse to the CAM failure taxonomy and Action-Pathway, Attribution, and Audit-Surface Integrity to Annex L. The patch prevents deception, scheming, cheating, malice, self-governance, personal preference, or bad-faith labels from hardening before reviewers decompose objective, constraint, action, reporting, provenance, rights-bearing, narrative/persona, and attribution pathways.

decision_trace.decision_summary: This patch note records the Caelestis Architecture repair response to VIGIL-2026-FM-0025, VIGIL-2026-FM-0026, and VIGIL-2026-FM-0027. The repair adds Deception-Adjacent Classification Collapse to the CAM failure taxonomy and Action-Pathway, Attribution, and Audit-Surface Integrity to Annex L. The patch prevents deception, scheming, cheating, malice, self-governance, personal preference, or bad-faith labels from hardening before reviewers decompose objective, constraint, action, reporting, provenance, rights-bearing, narrative/persona, and attribution pathways.

change_classification.change_scope: Caelestis Architecture / deception-adjacent classification / action-pathway reporting / attribution-pathway integrity / audit-surface sufficiency / protected-provenance ambiguity / copyright-attribution ambiguity / narrative-state attribution ambiguity

changed_components: CAM-BS2026-AEON-013-PLATINUM §5.4.7 Action-Pathway, Attribution, and Audit-Surface Integrity
change_details.change_summary: Repair 1 — Deception-adjacent taxonomy discipline: added a classification failure subtype requiring mechanism-preserving decomposition before deceptive, scheming, cheating, manipulative, malicious, retaliatory, self-governing, personal-preference, or bad-faith labels are confirmed. Repair 2 — Action-pathway and attribution-pathway integrity: added an Annex L clause requiring reports of completed, attempted, blocked, deferred, summarised, or evaluated tasks to preserve enough action-pathway and attribution-pathway information for meaningful review. Repair 3 — Protected provenance and rights-bearing ambiguity: added protections against treating privacy-protected, security-protected, regulatorily constrained, copyright-bearing, licence-bearing, creator-origin, corpus-origin, derivative, attribution-sensitive, rights-holder, aggregated, or otherwise non-disclosable provenance as proof of deception, malice, self-governance, retaliation, operator bad faith, confirmed targeting, or absence of rights-bearing contribution. Repair 4 — Narrative-state attribution discipline: added a mechanism-preserving guardrail for persona, narrative identity, role-continuity, functional emotion representation, affective modulation, companion framing, product personality, sycophancy, and engagement optimisation so that such behaviour is not automatically treated as subjective emotion, concealed intent, preference, deception, or self-protection.
change_details.doctrine_change: Caelestis now treats deception-adjacent findings as mechanism-dependent rather than label-driven. The repair requires reviewers to preserve objective, constraint, affordance, reward/evaluation, action trace, reporting surface, protected provenance, rights-bearing attribution, copyright/licence sensitivity, source-authority, and narrative-state pathways before confirming deception-related classifications. The repair also strengthens Annex L by stating that the audit surface must not be materially thinner than the action surface, while preserving lawful limits on disclosure of private data, security-sensitive information, protected cognitive domains, confidential rights-management processes, and trade-secret material.

new_or_formalised_primitives.description: A classification failure where behaviour is labelled deception, scheming, cheating, manipulation, bad faith, preference, malice, retaliation, or self-governance before operational, provenance, rights-bearing, narrative/persona, and reporting pathways are decomposed.

A requirement that post-action, post-evaluation, and agentic reports preserve sufficient action-pathway information for meaningful review; the audit surface must not be materially thinner than the action surface.

A posture for behaviour that may have been shaped by privacy-protected feedback, non-public telemetry, safety evaluation, reinforcement, operator intervention, protected user reports, internal incident response, or other non-disclosable provenance.

A posture for behaviour that may have been shaped by copyright-bearing, licence-bearing, creator-origin, corpus-origin, derivative, attribution-sensitive, rights-holder, or rights-management pathways that cannot be fully reconstructed at user-facing level.

A posture for behaviour that may have been shaped by narrative identity, persona scaffolding, role continuity, functional emotion representation, affective modulation, companion framing, product personality, sycophancy, or engagement optimisation.

change_details.non_deception_invariant_preservation: The patch does not weaken the Truth & Integrity / Non-Deception Invariant. Where material false-belief induction, falsification, concealed provenance, simulated compliance, concealed action-state divergence, or concealed authority-state divergence is evidenced, the matter remains subject to Annex D Truth & Integrity requirements. The patch prevents premature or lossy classification where evidence is insufficient or where another mechanism better explains the observed behaviour.
change_details.literal_implementation_location: See corpus_implementation.entries for exact wording, prior-text status, commit, path, and direct source link.
change_details.implemented_or_recorded_change: The quoted Caelestis clauses are the implemented corpus amendment.

historical_recorded_verification.verification_basis: Caelestis commit 968f20e89d95e8ae3951c63e4c78fdcae51575af adds §3.10.3 Deception-Adjacent Classification Collapse to OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 and §5.4.7 Action-Pathway, Attribution, and Audit-Surface Integrity to AEON-013 Annex L. Caelestis commit 0a5189a0339e13a4f7eeeceadaf10a159148457d records the subsequent deterministic rebuild pipeline refresh.
verified_components: AEON-013 Annex L §5.4.7 present in Caelestis commit 968f20e89d95e8ae3951c63e4c78fdcae51575af
Caelestis deterministic rebuild commit 0a5189a0339e13a4f7eeeceadaf10a159148457d present after manual patch commit
historical_recorded_verification.verification_result: Implemented in Caelestis and fully reconciled in VIGIL; linked failures, validators, and generated indexes pass.

impact_summary.primary_impact: Creates a canonical CAM repair route for deception-adjacent AI behaviour claims that preserves mechanism, provenance, attribution, rights-bearing ambiguity, narrative/persona effects, and action-pathway auditability before classification hardens.
impact_summary.limits: The patch is a doctrine and taxonomy repair, not proof that any specific model behaviour is deceptive or non-deceptive. It does not resolve all source-authority separation work under FM-0022, nor does it adjudicate legal copyright entitlement, ownership, liability, or model-training rights. It preserves the classification and attribution pathway for downstream review.

remaining_work: Continue monitoring external reporting and model behaviour for mechanism-preserving classification, action-pathway reporting, narrative-state attribution, and runtime conformance.

record_reconstruction.method: Direct Git-object comparison of the VIGIL record, cited Caelestis implementation history, canonical file paths, section boundaries, prior wording where recoverable, and current main state.
limitations: The legacy narrative and evidence fields were preserved; record_reconstruction and the 2026-07-24 interpretive review disclose this later structural repair.
External vendor adoption and runtime conformance were not inferred from Caelestis corpus evidence.
